reluctant.definition: Unwilling and hesitant to do something.
reluctant.example: She was reluctant to lend him her car because of his poor driving history.

diligent.definition: Working carefully and with a lot of effort.
diligent.example: He is a diligent student who always completes his homework on time.

benevolent.definition: Kind, helpful and wanting to do good for others.
benevolent.example: The hospital was built with support from a benevolent local citizen.

candid.definition: Honest and direct, even when the truth is uncomfortable.
candid.example: To be candid with you, I do not think this design will appeal to our target audience.

abundant.definition: Existing in large quantities; more than enough.
abundant.example: The forest offers an abundant supply of fresh water and edible plants.

generous.definition: Happy to give more money, help or time than expected.
generous.example: It was very generous of them to donate so much to the charity.

exhausted.definition: Extremely tired, with no energy left.
exhausted.example: After the long hike, everyone was completely exhausted.

convenient.definition: Easy to use or reach; causing no difficulty.
convenient.example: The new supermarket is very convenient because it is close to my house.

curious.definition: Eager to know or learn about something.
curious.example: The curious child kept asking questions about how the machine worked.

fragile.example: Please handle the package carefully; the glass items inside are fragile.

acquire.definition: To get or learn something over time.
acquire.example: Children acquire language skills rapidly during their early years.

advocate.definition: To publicly support an idea, plan or cause.
advocate.example: Medical professionals advocate getting at least seven hours of sleep per night.

empirical.definition: Based on observation or experiment, not only on theory.
empirical.example: The researcher presented empirical evidence to support her hypothesis.

fluctuate.definition: To rise and fall irregularly in amount or level.
fluctuate.example: The stock market tends to fluctuate wildly in response to political news.

scrutinize.definition: To examine something very carefully and closely.
scrutinize.example: Every line of the contract was closely scrutinized by the company's legal team.

hypothesis.definition: An idea that is suggested as a possible explanation and can be tested.
hypothesis.example: The scientists designed an experiment to test their hypothesis.

coherent.example: She gave a coherent explanation of the complex process.

significant.definition: Large or important enough to be noticed.
significant.example: There was a significant improvement in the patient's condition.

capricious.definition: Changing mood or behavior suddenly and unpredictably.
capricious.example: The capricious weather went from sunny to a severe thunderstorm in minutes.

loquacious.example: The loquacious host kept his guests entertained with endless stories.

ephemeral.definition: Lasting for only a very short time.
ephemeral.example: Fame in the internet age is often ephemeral, lasting only a few days.

pragmatic.definition: Dealing with problems in a practical, realistic way.
pragmatic.example: We need to take a pragmatic approach to the budget problem rather than fighting over theories.

cacophony.definition: A loud, unpleasant mixture of sounds.
cacophony.example: A cacophony of car horns and construction noise flooded through the open window.

ubiquitous.definition: Seeming to be everywhere at the same time.
ubiquitous.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern society.

meticulous.definition: Giving great attention to every small detail.
meticulous.example: The editor was meticulous about checking every detail of the manuscript.

ambiguous.definition: Having more than one possible meaning; unclear.
ambiguous.example: His ambiguous answer left everyone confused about his real intentions.

leverage.definition: To use something you have to gain an advantage.
leverage.example: We should leverage our strong brand reputation to enter the new market.

redundant.definition: No longer needed or useful; extra.
redundant.example: Due to the merger, fifty employees were unfortunately made redundant.

lucrative.definition: Producing a lot of money or profit.
lucrative.example: She left her teaching job to start a highly lucrative consulting firm.

stagnant.example: Wage growth has remained stagnant for the past five years despite inflation.

mitigate.definition: To make something less harmful or serious.
mitigate.example: Good planning can help mitigate the negative effects of the economic downturn.

negotiate.definition: To discuss something to reach an agreement.
negotiate.example: The union will negotiate with management over the new contract terms.

revenue.definition: The money a company or government receives.
revenue.example: The company's revenue grew by fifteen percent last quarter.

outsource.definition: To pay another company to do part of your work.
outsource.example: Many companies outsource their customer support to reduce costs.

happy.definition: Feeling or showing pleasure and joy.
happy.example: The children were very happy at the park.

brave.definition: Showing courage; not afraid of danger.
brave.example: The brave firefighter rescued the cat from the tree.

tiny.example: A tiny kitten was sleeping in the basket.

enormous.definition: Extremely large in size or amount.
enormous.example: They live in an enormous house near the sea.

noisy.definition: Making a lot of loud sound.
noisy.example: The classroom was too noisy to study in.

quiet.example: We found a quiet corner in the library.

wealthy.definition: Having a lot of money or possessions.
wealthy.example: A wealthy businessman donated money to the school.

ancient.definition: Very old; from a long time ago.
ancient.example: We visited an ancient temple during our trip.

modern.definition: Of the present time; new in style.
modern.example: The museum is in a very modern building.

poor.definition: Having very little money, possessions, or resources.
poor.example: He grew up in a poor neighborhood.

absolute.definition: Complete and total; not limited in any way.

additional.example: There is an additional fee for this service.

adequate.definition: Enough for a particular need or purpose.

anxious.example: She felt anxious before the exam.

apparent.example: It is apparent that he is tired.

appropriate.definition: Suitable or fit for a particular purpose.
appropriate.example: This is the most appropriate solution.

available.definition: Ready for use or able to be obtained.

average.definition: Typical or usual; not better or worse than most.

aware.example: I wasn't aware of the problem.

bad.definition: Of low quality or not good.

better.definition: Of a higher quality or standard.
better.example: This book is better than that one.

brief.example: Please give me a brief summary.

bright.definition: Emitting or reflecting a lot of light.
bright.example: The sun is very bright today.

capable.example: She is capable of great things.

central.example: The park is in the central part.

chemical.example: He works in a chemical lab.

clear.definition: Easy to see, hear or understand.

close.example: The school is close to my house.

competitive.definition: Wanting to be better than others.

concerned.example: I am concerned about her health.

confident.example: He is confident in his abilities.

considerable.example: He earned a considerable amount of money.

consistent.definition: Always behaving or happening in the same way.

constant.definition: Happening all the time without stopping.
constant.example: The machine makes a constant noise.

contemporary.example: We visited a contemporary art museum.

conventional.definition: Following what is traditional or usual.
conventional.example: He wore a conventional dark suit.

creative.definition: Good at producing new and original ideas or things.

criminal.definition: Relating to crime or illegal acts.

cultural.definition: Relating to culture, art and traditions.
cultural.example: The city has a rich cultural life.

daily.example: This is part of my daily routine.

dangerous.definition: Able or likely to cause harm.

dependent.definition: Needing someone or something for support.
dependent.example: The baby is dependent on its mother.

desperate.definition: Feeling hopeless and willing to try anything.
desperate.example: He was desperate for a job.

determined.definition: Having a strong will to do something.

disabled.definition: Having a condition that limits some activities.
disabled.example: The building has access for disabled people.

distinct.example: The two flavors are quite distinct.

domestic.definition: Relating to home or one's own country.

early.definition: Before the usual or expected time.
early.example: I woke up early this morning.

eastern.example: They live in the eastern part of the city.

efficient.definition: Working well without wasting time or energy.
efficient.example: The new engine is very efficient.

enthusiastic.example: She is enthusiastic about her new job.

existing.example: We will improve the existing system.

experimental.definition: Based on new ideas that are being tested.

extensive.definition: Covering a large area or amount.

extreme.definition: Very great in degree; far from moderate.

famous.example: Istanbul is famous for its history.

far.example: The station is not far from here.

few.example: Few people came to the meeting.

former.definition: Existing or holding a position in the past.
former.example: The former president gave a speech.

free.example: Entry to the museum is free.

frequent.example: He makes frequent visits to his family.

general.definition: Relating to the whole, not the details.

genuine.definition: Real and exactly what it appears to be.
genuine.example: This bag is made of genuine leather.

global.example: Climate change is a global problem.

grateful.example: I am grateful for your help.

great.example: We had a great time at the party.

heavy.example: This box is too heavy to carry.

high.definition: Reaching far up from the ground.

huge.example: They live in a huge house.

ill.example: She stayed home because she was ill.

impossible.definition: Not able to happen or be done.
impossible.example: It is impossible to finish today.

impressive.example: The view from the tower is impressive.

independent.example: She is a very independent person.

individual.example: Each individual student gets a book.

interested.definition: Wanting to know more about something.

internal.example: The company held an internal meeting.

international.example: She works for an international company.

involved.example: He is involved in many projects.

keen.example: She is keen to learn English.

known.example: The area is known for its beaches.

left.definition: On the side of the body where the heart is.

likely.example: It is likely to rain tomorrow.

little.example: A little girl was playing in the garden.

local.example: We ate at a local restaurant.

lonely.example: He felt lonely in the big city.

long.definition: Measuring a great distance or time.

quickly.definition: At a fast speed; in a short time.
quickly.example: She quickly finished her homework before dinner.

slowly.definition: At a slow speed; not fast.
slowly.example: The old man walked slowly across the street.

carefully.definition: With great attention, avoiding mistakes or damage.
carefully.example: Please read the instructions carefully before starting.

always.definition: At all times; on every occasion.
always.example: She always drinks tea in the morning.

never.definition: Not at any time; not ever.

often.example: We often visit our grandparents on weekends.

sometimes.definition: On some occasions but not always.
sometimes.example: Sometimes I take the bus instead of walking.

loudly.definition: In a way that makes a lot of noise.
loudly.example: The fans cheered loudly when the team scored.

quietly.example: She quietly closed the door so the baby could sleep.

suddenly.example: Suddenly, the lights went out in the whole house.

journey.definition: The act of travelling from one place to another.
journey.example: The journey to the mountain village took five hours.

knowledge.definition: Information and understanding gained through learning.
knowledge.example: Reading books is a great way to gain knowledge.

courage.definition: The ability to face fear or difficulty bravely.
courage.example: It takes courage to speak in front of a large crowd.

friendship.example: Their friendship started in primary school.

weather.definition: The condition of the air: rain, sun, wind, etc.
weather.example: The weather was perfect for a picnic yesterday.

health.definition: The condition of your body and mind.
health.example: Regular exercise is important for your health.

success.definition: Achieving what you wanted or planned.
success.example: Hard work is the key to success.

mistake.example: Everyone makes a mistake from time to time.

opportunity.definition: A chance to do something good or useful.
opportunity.example: This job is a great opportunity for young graduates.

memory.definition: Something you remember, or the ability to remember.
memory.example: That summer holiday is my favorite childhood memory.

go.definition: To move or travel to a place. (go – went – gone)
go.example: We go to the gym twice a week.

eat.definition: To put food in your mouth and swallow it. (eat – ate – eaten)
eat.example: They usually eat breakfast at seven o'clock.

see.definition: To notice with your eyes. (see – saw – seen)
see.example: You can see the mountains from this window.

drink.definition: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it. (drink – drank – drunk)
drink.example: I drink a glass of water every morning.

write.definition: To form letters or words on paper or a screen. (write – wrote – written)
write.example: Students write an essay every Friday.

speak.definition: To say words; to talk. (speak – spoke – spoken)
speak.example: She can speak three languages fluently.

swim.definition: To move through water using your body. (swim – swam – swum)
swim.example: The children swim in the lake every summer.

fly.definition: To move through the air. (fly – flew – flown)
fly.example: Some birds fly south for the winter.

break.definition: To separate into pieces by force. (break – broke – broken)
break.example: Be careful not to break the glass vase.

buy.definition: To get something by paying money. (buy – bought – bought)
buy.example: We buy fresh bread from the bakery every day.

loose.definition: Not firmly held or tightly in place
loose.example: The screw on the door handle is loose.

lost.example: I hope they find the lost dog soon.

lower.definition: Less high in position or status
lower.example: Please sit on the lower step of the stairs.

main.example: What is the main reason for your visit?

major.example: The construction caused a major delay in traffic.

many.example: There are many students in the classroom today.

medical.example: He needs urgent medical attention after the accident.

medieval.example: They visited a medieval castle during their trip.

mental.example: Chess requires a lot of mental effort.

middle.example: She sat in the middle seat of the car.

military.definition: Relating to soldiers or armed forces
military.example: He wore a green military uniform to the event.

mixed.definition: Consisting of different qualities or things
mixed.example: They served a bowl of mixed nuts at the party.

much.example: Do you have much work to finish today?

nasty.example: He has a nasty habit of biting his nails.

national.example: Today is a national holiday in our country.

native.definition: Associated with the place of one's birth
native.example: She is a native speaker of Spanish.

natural.definition: Existing in or caused by nature
natural.example: They prefer to eat natural food without chemicals.

naughty.example: The naughty boy drew on the living room wall.

near.example: We hope to visit them in the near future.

necessary.definition: Needed to achieve a particular result.
necessary.example: It is necessary to wear a helmet while riding.

nervous.example: She felt nervous before her big exam.

new.example: I want to buy a new car next year.

next.definition: Coming immediately after the present one.
next.example: We will meet again next week.

numerous.example: There are numerous reasons to exercise daily.

obvious.definition: Easy to see, recognize, or understand.
obvious.example: The answer to this question is obvious.

occasional.example: He enjoys an occasional walk in the park.

old.definition: Having lived or existed for a long time.
old.example: This old book belongs to my grandfather.

ordinary.definition: Not different or special in any way.
ordinary.example: It was just an ordinary day at work.

original.definition: Existing from the beginning; not copied.
original.example: This is the original copy of the document.

other.definition: As well as the one already mentioned.
other.example: Do you have any other questions?

particular.definition: Special or specific to a person or thing.
particular.example: Is there a particular reason you are late?

patient.definition: Able to wait without becoming angry.
patient.example: You need to be patient with young children.

peaceful.definition: Quiet and free from war or worry.
peaceful.example: The lake is very peaceful in the morning.

permanent.definition: Lasting for a long time or forever.
permanent.example: They are looking for a permanent home.

personal.definition: Relating to a particular person's private life.
personal.example: Please do not ask me personal questions.

pleasant.example: We had a pleasant conversation yesterday.

plenty.definition: Available in large or sufficient quantities.
plenty.example: Five dollars is plenty for a simple lunch.

political.definition: Relating to the government or public affairs.
political.example: She decided to start a political career.

popular.definition: Liked or enjoyed by many people.
popular.example: Football is a very popular sport worldwide.

possible.definition: Able to be done or achieved.
possible.example: It is possible to complete the work today.

potential.definition: Having the capacity to develop in the future.
potential.example: She is a potential candidate for the job.

powerful.definition: Having great power, strength, or influence.
powerful.example: The president gave a powerful speech.

practical.definition: Relating to actual experience rather than theory.
practical.example: We need a practical solution to this problem.

precise.definition: Exact, accurate, and careful about details.
precise.example: Please give me the precise measurements.

present.example: The whole team is present at the meeting.

previous.definition: Existing or happening before something else.
previous.example: She has experience from her previous job.

primary.example: Our primary goal is to improve customer service.

private.definition: For the use of a single person or group.
private.example: This is a private area for members only.

proper.example: It is important to use the proper tool.

public.definition: Relating to or available to people in general.
public.example: You can find information in the public library.

pure.definition: Not mixed with any other substance.
pure.example: This ring is made of pure gold.

rapid.definition: Happening or moving with great speed.
rapid.example: The city is experiencing rapid growth.

rare.example: It is rare to see snow here.

real.example: This is real leather, not synthetic.

reasonable.example: The hotel offers service at a reasonable price.

recent.definition: Happening or starting only a short time ago.
recent.example: A recent study shows that exercise improves memory.

regular.example: You should do regular exercise to stay healthy.

related.example: The two events are related to each other.

relevant.definition: Closely connected to the matter at hand.
relevant.example: Please provide the relevant documents for your application.

religious.definition: Relating to religion or believing in a god.
religious.example: She is a very religious person who goes to church daily.

remaining.definition: Still left after other parts have gone.
remaining.example: The remaining students must finish their work now.

remarkable.example: He has a remarkable ability to remember faces.

responsible.definition: Having control or authority over something.
responsible.example: You are responsible for keeping your room clean.

ridiculous.example: It is ridiculous to pay so much for water.

right.example: I hope we made the right decision today.

rural.example: They prefer to live in a rural area.

same.example: We went to the same school together.

satisfied.definition: Pleased because you got what you wanted.
satisfied.example: I am fully satisfied with my new job.

scientific.definition: Relating to science or systematic study.
scientific.example: They need strong scientific proof before they believe it.

senior.definition: Having a higher rank or status.
senior.example: She is a senior manager at the company.

sensible.example: Wearing a warm coat is a sensible choice.

sensitive.example: Her skin is very sensitive to sunlight.

separate.example: We would like to have separate rooms please.

serious.example: Please pay attention to this serious matter.

severe.example: The region is facing a severe water shortage.

short.definition: Measuring a small distance or time.
short.example: She wrote a short story for the competition.

similar.definition: Look or behave almost the same.
similar.example: These two cars are very similar in design.

simple.example: The rules of the game are very simple.

slow.definition: Moving or happening at low speed.
slow.example: The service is very slow here.

small.example: They live in a small house.

sole.example: He is the sole survivor of the crash.

sorry.example: I am sorry for the delay.

special.definition: Better, greater, or otherwise different from what is usual.
special.example: This is a very special occasion.

specific.example: I need a specific tool for this job.

spiritual.definition: Relating to the human spirit or soul.

stable.definition: Not likely to change or fail.
stable.example: The economy remains stable this year.

straightforward.example: The instructions are simple and straightforward.

strict.example: Our school has a strict dress code.

strong.definition: Having great power or physical strength.
strong.example: She has a strong work ethic.

subsequent.example: The subsequent events proved him right.

substantial.definition: Large in size, value, or importance.
substantial.example: She made a substantial contribution to the project.

successful.example: He is a very successful businessman.

sufficient.example: We have sufficient evidence to prove it.

suitable.definition: Right or appropriate for a particular purpose.
suitable.example: This dress is suitable for a wedding.

sure.example: I am sure about my decision.

surprised.example: He looked surprised when he heard the news.

surprising.example: It is surprising that they lost the game.

temporary.definition: Lasting for only a limited time.
temporary.example: This office is just a temporary solution.

total.example: The project was a total failure.

traditional.example: They wore traditional clothes for the festival.

true.example: This is a true story about love.

ultimate.example: Winning the cup is our ultimate goal.

unfair.example: The rules of this game are unfair.

unique.definition: Being the only one of its kind.
unique.example: Every person has a unique voice.

unknown.example: They traveled to an unknown island.

unlikely.example: Such a mistake is unlikely to happen again.

urban.definition: Relating to a town or city.
urban.example: I prefer urban life to the countryside.

urgent.example: She needs to make an urgent call.

useful.definition: Helping to do or achieve something.
useful.example: This book is a useful guide for beginners.

various.example: We can use various methods to solve this.

vast.definition: Very great in size or amount.
vast.example: They walked across the vast desert.

very.example: This is the very book I wanted to buy.

violent.example: We must avoid a violent conflict.

vital.example: Water is vital for all living things.

voluntary.definition: Done by choice without being forced or paid.
voluntary.example: She did some voluntary work at the local hospital.

vulnerable.definition: Easy to hurt physically or emotionally.
vulnerable.example: Small children are vulnerable to many diseases.

weak.example: His legs felt weak after running the marathon.

well.definition: Healthy and in good physical condition.
well.example: I hope you feel well today.

western.definition: Located in or directed toward the west.
western.example: They live in the western part of the city.

wide.definition: Measuring a large distance from side to side.
wide.example: The river is very wide near the ocean.

widespread.definition: Existing or happening in many places.
widespread.example: There is widespread support for the new law.

willing.definition: Ready or pleased to do something.
willing.example: He is willing to help us clean the house.

wise.definition: Having deep knowledge and good judgment.
wise.example: She gave me some wise advice about my future.

wrong.example: It is wrong to lie to your friends.

young.definition: Having lived or existed for only a short time.
young.example: The young boy loves to play outside.

indifferent.definition: Having no particular interest or sympathy.
indifferent.example: He is indifferent to politics and never votes.

growing.definition: Increasing in size, amount, or degree.
growing.example: There is a growing need for clean energy.

increased.definition: Greater in size, amount, or degree.
increased.example: We face increased pressure to finish the project.

several.definition: More than two but not many.
several.example: I have several questions about the plan.

further.example: Please contact us for further information.

beneficial.definition: Improving a situation or having a helpful effect.
beneficial.example: Fresh air is beneficial for your health.

expected.example: The expected arrival time of the train is noon.

reckless.definition: Doing something dangerous without caring about risks.
reckless.example: His reckless driving caused a bad accident.

appealing.example: The idea of a beach holiday sounds very appealing.

controversial.definition: Causing a lot of disagreement or discussion.
controversial.example: This is a controversial topic that people often argue about.

costly.example: Buying a new car can be a costly mistake.

novel.definition: New, original, and not like anything seen before.
novel.example: She thought of a novel way to solve the problem.

harsh.example: The winter weather can be very harsh in this region.

innovative.example: We need an innovative approach to improve our sales.

sizeable.definition: Fairly large in size or amount.
sizeable.example: The company received a sizeable amount of money from investors.

constructive.definition: Useful and intended to help or improve.
constructive.example: Please give me some constructive feedback on my work.

destructive.example: High winds can have a destructive effect on old buildings.

progressive.definition: Favoring social reform or new, liberal ideas.
progressive.example: The school uses a progressive method to teach young children.

loyal.definition: Remaining constant in support or friendship.
loyal.example: A loyal dog will always stay by your side.

profitable.definition: Making or likely to make money.
profitable.example: The small business became profitable after its second year.

favorable.definition: Showing approval or giving an advantage.
favorable.example: The conditions are favorable for starting a new project.

sophisticated.definition: Having a lot of experience and knowledge of culture.
sophisticated.example: He is a sophisticated man who enjoys classical music.

variable.definition: Likely to change or vary frequently.
variable.example: The weather here is highly variable during the spring.

fertile.definition: Producing many plants or crops easily.
fertile.example: Farmers love this region because of its fertile soil.

rising.definition: Going up or increasing in level or amount.
rising.example: High demand is the main cause of rising prices.

adverse.example: Bad weather can have an adverse effect on your health.

excessive.definition: More than is necessary, normal, or desirable.
excessive.example: Please avoid excessive noise during the late evening hours.

representative.definition: Typical of a class, group, or body of opinion.
representative.example: We need a representative sample of the whole population.

respective.definition: Belonging or relating separately to each of two or more
respective.example: They both went back to their respective homes after work.

influential.definition: Having a lot of influence on someone or something
influential.example: She is a very influential leader in this business industry.

drastic.definition: Likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect
drastic.example: The company had to make a drastic change to survive.

profound.example: Her speech had a profound impact on the entire audience.

harmful.definition: Causing or likely to cause damage or harm
harmful.example: Smoking is extremely harmful to your health.

detrimental.definition: Tending to cause harm or damage
detrimental.example: Lack of sleep can have a detrimental effect on brain.

worldwide.definition: Extending or reaching throughout the entire world
worldwide.example: The company has achieved worldwide success with this new app.

trivial.example: We should not waste time on such a trivial matter.

developing.example: This program aims to support several developing nations.

developed.definition: Advanced in technologies and economic status
developed.example: Most developed countries offer high standards of public healthcare.

prevalent.definition: Widespread or common in a particular area
prevalent.example: Flu is very prevalent during the cold winter months.

reliable.definition: Consistently good in quality or performance
reliable.example: It is hard to find a truly reliable car nowadays.

unreliable.definition: Not able to be trusted or depended on
unreliable.example: Public transport in this town can be very unreliable sometimes.

exceptional.example: The young musician showed an exceptional talent for playing piano.

resistant.definition: Offering resistance to something or unaffected by it
resistant.example: This new watch is completely resistant to water.

affordable.example: We need to find an affordable apartment near the city.

underlying.definition: Basic, fundamental, or hidden beneath the surface
underlying.example: Stress was the underlying cause of his constant headaches.

valid.example: You must present a valid passport at the airport gate.

competent.definition: Having the necessary ability or skill to do something
competent.example: She is a highly competent attorney with years of experience.

delicate.example: The antique vase is very delicate.

exclusive.definition: Only for a limited or select group.
exclusive.example: They belong to an exclusive club in the city.

extravagant.definition: Spending much more money than is necessary.
extravagant.example: She has very extravagant tastes in fashion.

frustrated.definition: Feeling annoyed because you cannot achieve goals.
frustrated.example: He felt frustrated because the computer was slow.

gradual.definition: Happening slowly over a period of time.
gradual.example: We noticed a gradual change in the weather.

feasible.definition: Possible and likely to be achieved easily.
feasible.example: His plan is not feasible within our budget.

notorious.example: The area is notorious for high crime rates.

respectable.example: He comes from a highly respectable family.

subtle.definition: Not loud, bright, noticeable, or obvious.
subtle.example: There is a subtle difference between the two colors.

susceptible.definition: Easily influenced or harmed by something.
susceptible.example: Young children are very susceptible to infections.

selective.definition: Intentional in choosing only certain things.
selective.example: The university is highly selective in its admissions.

arrogant.definition: Behaving in an unpleasant, proud way.
arrogant.example: He is so arrogant that he never listens.

decisive.definition: Strongly affecting how a situation will progress.
decisive.example: We need a decisive leader to win this election.

declining.example: The company faces a declining market for its products.

disturbing.definition: Making you feel worried or upset.

deceptive.definition: Making you believe something that is not true.

predictable.definition: Happening in a way that is expected.

ingenious.example: She came up with an ingenious solution to the problem.

immense.definition: Extremely large or great, especially in scale or degree.

implicit.example: There was an implicit agreement between them.

explicit.definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion.

noticeable.example: There was a noticeable change in his behavior.

protective.definition: Intended to protect someone or something.

preventive.definition: Designed to keep something undesirable from occurring.

notable.definition: Worthy of attention or notice; remarkable.

regrettable.definition: Making you feel sad or sorry about something.

deficient.definition: Not having enough of a specified quality or ingredient.

abrupt.example: The train came to an abrupt stop.

desirable.example: Good communication skills are highly desirable for this job.

irreversible.definition: Impossible to change back to a previous state.

negligible.definition: So small or unimportant that it can be ignored.

durable.definition: Remaining in good condition for a long time.

preferable.definition: More desirable or suitable than alternatives.

random.definition: Done or chosen without a plan or pattern.

predominant.definition: Being most common, frequent, or prominent.

credible.definition: Able to be believed or trusted.

obscure.definition: Not known to many people or difficult to understand.

inaccessible.definition: Very difficult or impossible to reach.

relaxed.example: She felt completely relaxed after her long vacation.

diffident.definition: Shy and lacking confidence in one's own ability.

disposable.definition: Designed to be thrown away after use.

longer.definition: Measuring more in length or time.

solar.definition: Relating to or determined by the sun.

increasing.definition: Becoming greater in size, amount, or degree.

overwhelming.definition: Very great in amount or strength.

indispensable.example: A good dictionary is indispensable for language learning.

mutual.definition: Shared by two or more people.

indicative.definition: Serving as a sign or indication of something.

inhospitable.definition: Harsh and difficult to live in.

dazzling.example: She wore a dazzling diamond necklace to the party.

poignant.definition: Evoking a keen sense of sadness or regret.

preservative.definition: Having the power to keep something safe from decay.

optional.definition: Left to one's choice; not mandatory.

faulty.definition: Not working correctly; having a defect.

compulsive.definition: Resulting from or relating to an irresistible urge.

probable.definition: Likely to happen or be true.

irrelevant.definition: Not related to the subject being discussed.

avoidable.example: Most of these accidents are avoidable if you are careful.

randomly.definition: Without a definite aim or pattern.
randomly.example: The computer picked a winner randomly.

adversely.example: Bad weather can adversely affect crop growth.

formerly.example: Istanbul was formerly known as Constantinople.

solely.definition: Not involving anyone or anything else.
solely.example: He is solely responsible for the project.

remarkably.definition: In a way that is worthy of attention.
remarkably.example: He recovered remarkably fast from the illness.

conversely.definition: Introducing a statement that is the opposite.
conversely.example: City life is fast; conversely, country life is slow.

dramatically.definition: By a large amount or to a great extent.
